isolation.scope: iOS source under apps/ios/Sources/Litter/ and tests under apps/ios/Tests/LitterTests/

steps.expected: AgentPickerSheet iterates over detected agents from input, no hardcoded list
steps.observed: AgentPickerSheet takes `agentInfos: [AgentInfo]` parameter. Body uses `ForEach(agentInfos, id: \.id)` to iterate all detected agents dynamically. No hardcoded agent list found.

steps.action: Verify dynamic agent list construction in DiscoveryView
steps.expected: Picker receives agents from discovery/detection data
steps.observed: DiscoveryView constructs pickerInfos from runtime-detected agents (agentPickerDetectedAgents), server.agentInfos, or synthesized from server.agentTypes. All paths are dynamic.

steps.expected: AgentPickerSheet handles .genericAcp agent type with gearshape icon and ACP label
steps.observed: AgentPickerSheet has a dedicated ACP Profiles section (`acpProfilesSection`) that shows when `genericAcpAvailable` is true. GenericAcp rows show gearshape.fill icon (AgentType.genericAcp.icon = "gearshape.fill") and "ACP" capsule badge. Custom command editor allows configuring remote command before connecting.

steps.action: Verify AgentType.genericAcp has correct icon and label
steps.expected: gearshape icon and 'ACP' label
steps.observed: AgentBadgeView.swift: genericAcp.icon = "gearshape.fill", genericAcp.displayName = "ACP", genericAcp.tintColor = Color.gray. In picker rows, ACP badge shows "ACP" text with Capsule background.

steps.action: Check InlineAgentSelectorView for transport compatibility filtering
steps.expected: Agents with unavailable transports are grayed out and disabled
steps.observed: InlineAgentSelectorView has `isCompatible(_ info:)` method that checks `!Set(info.detectedTransports).isDisjoint(with: Set(availableTransports))`. Incompatible agents have opacity 0.4, 'Transport unavailable' text in LitterTheme.danger color, and `.disabled(!compatible)` modifier. Compatible agents show transport labels.

codeReferences: apps/ios/Sources/Litter/Views/AgentPickerView.swift: isCompatible method, agentRow — opacity/disabled/foregroundColor conditional on compatible

assertions.title: Agent selection persists across app launches

steps.expected: Selection persisted via UserDefaults
steps.observed: AgentSelectionStore uses `UserDefaults.standard` with key pattern `litter.selectedAgent.<serverId>`. setSelectedAgentType writes string via `defaults.set(agentType.persistentKey, forKey:)`. selectedAgentType reads via `defaults.string(forKey:)`. Both Codable extension and persistentKey/fromPersistentKey provide stable serialization. Tests confirm round-trip persistence.

steps.expected: Tests validate persistence behavior
steps.observed: AgentBadgePickerTests: testAgentSelectionPersistedToUserDefaults, testAgentSelectionSurvivesMultipleWrites, testAgentTypeCodableRoundTrip, testAgentTypePersistentKeys — all confirm persistence works.

assertions.title: Auto-selection when only one agent is available

steps.expected: effectiveAgentType auto-selects when only one agent available
steps.observed: AgentSelectionStore.effectiveAgentType(for:availableAgents:) checks: (1) if user has stored selection and it's in availableAgents, return it; (2) if availableAgents.count == 1, auto-select that agent AND persist it via setSelectedAgentType; (3) otherwise return nil. DiscoveryView also has auto-select logic: `if compatibleTypes.count == 1 { AgentSelectionStore.shared.setSelectedAgentType(compatibleTypes.first, for: server.id) }`.

steps.expected: Tests confirm auto-select behavior
steps.observed: AgentBadgePickerTests: testEffectiveAgentTypeAutoSelectsSingleAgent confirms single agent auto-selected and persisted. testEffectiveAgentTypeReturnsNilForMultipleAgents confirms nil when multiple available.

codeReferences: apps/ios/Sources/Litter/Views/AgentPickerView.swift: effectiveAgentType method — auto-select branch when count == 1

assertions.title: Picker re-opens when selection is needed but missing

steps.action: Check DiscoveryView for picker presentation logic
steps.expected: Picker shows when multi-agent server has no stored selection
steps.observed: DiscoveryView has two picker presentation points: (1) Already-connected check: `let needsPicker = server.agentTypes.count > 1 && AgentSelectionStore.shared.selectedAgentType(for: server.id) == nil` → sets agentPickerServer. (2) Pre-connect check: `let needsPicker = compatibleTypes.count > 1 && AgentSelectionStore.shared.selectedAgentType(for: server.id) == nil` → sets agentPickerServer. Both require count > 1 AND no stored selection.

steps.expected: Picker also opens from runtime SSH detection when multiple agents found
steps.observed: onChange handler checks for pendingAgentSelection from runtime detection and opens picker: `if progress.pendingAgentSelection, !progress.detectedAgents.isEmpty` → sets agentPickerDetectedAgents and agentPickerServer.

steps.expected: Tests confirm shouldShowPicker logic
steps.observed: AgentBadgePickerTests: testShouldShowPickerForMultipleAgents — confirms true for 2+ agents. testShouldNotShowPickerForSingleAgent — confirms false for single agent.

assertions.title: Session filter row includes agent type filter

steps.action: Check sessionFilterRow for agent type filter chip
steps.expected: Filter row has agent type filter menu chip
steps.observed: SessionsScreen.sessionFilterRow includes a Menu with: 'All agents' option (sets agentTypeFilter = nil), plus Codex, Pi, Droid, ACP options. The chip shows dynamic icon and title based on agentTypeFilter state. Uses filterChip helper with icon parameter from agentTypeFilter?.icon ?? 'cpu'.

steps.expected: Tests confirm filter options include all types
steps.observed: SessionFilterAndSettingsTests: testAgentTypeFilterOptionsIncludeAllTypes verifies Codex, Pi, Droid, ACP. testAgentTypeFilterIncludesGenericAcp confirms genericAcp is in the filter types.

codeReferences: apps/ios/Sources/Litter/Views/SessionsScreen.swift: sessionFilterRow — agent type Menu with All/Codex/Pi/Droid/ACP

assertions.title: Agent type filter shows correct icon per type

steps.action: Check filter chip icon changes based on selected type
steps.expected: Filter chip dynamically shows icon matching the selected agent type
steps.observed: SessionFilterRow uses `icon: agentTypeFilter?.icon ?? "cpu"` in filterChip call. Each AgentType has a distinct icon: Codex='terminal.fill', Pi='brain', Droid='robot', ACP='gearshape.fill'. Title uses `agentTypeFilter?.displayName ?? "All agents"` — dynamic per selection.

steps.expected: Tests confirm icon/displayName correctness
steps.observed: SessionFilterAndSettingsTests: testFilterChipUsesAgentIcon, testFilterChipUsesAgentTintColor, testFilterChipShowsCorrectDisplayNameWhenActive — all pass.

codeReferences: apps/ios/Sources/Litter/Views/SessionsScreen.swift: sessionFilterRow — icon: agentTypeFilter?.icon ?? "cpu", title: agentTypeFilter?.displayName ?? "All agents"

assertions.title: Filtering by agent type returns only matching sessions

steps.action: Check SessionsDerivation for agent type filter predicate
steps.expected: Filter logic matches sessions by their server's selected agent type
steps.observed: SessionsDerivation.build filters with agentTypeFilter parameter. Logic: if agentTypeFilter is set, checks each thread's server — if the server has a selectedAgent matching the filter, include it; otherwise check if the server's detected types include the filter type. This is in the `filteredThreads` computation.

steps.expected: Tests confirm filtering returns only matching sessions
steps.observed: SessionFilterAndSettingsTests: testSessionsDerivationFiltersByAgentType — Pi filter returns only t1, Droid filter returns only t3, nil filter returns all 3. testFilteringByAgentTypeWithNoMatchReturnsEmpty — empty result for non-matching filter. testFilteringByAgentTypeWithGenericAcp — ACP filter returns only t1.

assertions.title: Session rows show agent type badge for non-Codex agents

steps.action: Check session row template for AgentTypePill
steps.expected: Session rows show AgentTypePill for non-Codex agents
steps.observed: SessionsScreen.sessionRow includes: `if let agentBadge = agentBadgeType(for: thread) { AgentTypePill(agentType: agentBadge) }`. The agentBadgeType helper checks AgentSelectionStore for the thread's server — returns the selected agent type if non-nil and non-Codex, returns nil for Codex (no badge).

steps.expected: Tests confirm badge appears for non-Codex, not for Codex
steps.observed: SessionFilterAndSettingsTests: testAgentBadgeTypeReturnsNonNilForNonCodexSelection — Pi returns non-nil badge. testAgentBadgeTypeReturnsNilForCodexSelection — no selection returns nil, explicit Codex returns .codex. testBadgeTypesForAllNonCodexAgents — all 5 non-Codex types verified.

codeReferences: apps/ios/Sources/Litter/Views/SessionsScreen.swift: sessionRow — `if let agentBadge = agentBadgeType(for: thread) { AgentTypePill(agentType: agentBadge) }`

assertions.title: Settings shows permission policy for each agent type

steps.action: Check SettingsView for agent permission policy section
steps.expected: Settings has per-agent permission policy rows
steps.observed: SettingsView.agentSettingsSection includes a ForEach over [Codex, Pi, Droid] agent types, each showing a Menu with the current permission policy. Menu options: Auto Approve All, Auto Reject High Risk, Prompt Always. Each row shows AgentBadgeView, agent name + 'Permissions', and current policy shortName.

steps.expected: Tests confirm per-agent permission policy persistence
steps.observed: SessionFilterAndSettingsTests: testPerAgentPermissionPolicyPersisted — sets different policies for Codex/Pi/Droid and verifies each independently persists. Defaults to promptAlways.

codeReferences: apps/ios/Sources/Litter/Views/SettingsView.swift: agentSettingsSection — ForEach Codex/Pi/Droid with permission policy Menu

assertions.title: Settings shows transport preference for Pi and Droid

steps.action: Check SettingsView for transport preference rows
steps.expected: Settings has transport preference rows for Pi and Droid
steps.observed: SettingsView.agentSettingsSection has second ForEach over [piNative, droidNative] with transport preference Menus. Pi options: Auto, Native (Pi RPC), ACP (via pi-acp). Droid options: Auto, Native (Factory API), ACP (via droid exec). Each row shows transport icon, agent name + 'Transport', and current preference label.

steps.expected: Tests confirm transport preference persistence for Pi and Droid
steps.observed: SessionFilterAndSettingsTests: testTransportPreferencePersistedForPi — round-trips piNative/piAcp/nil. testTransportPreferencePersistedForDroid — round-trips droidNative/droidAcp.

codeReferences: apps/ios/Sources/Litter/Views/SettingsView.swift: agentSettingsSection — ForEach [piNative, droidNative] transport preference Menu

assertions.title: Permission policy changes take effect immediately

steps.expected: Permission changes write to UserDefaults synchronously and are observable
steps.observed: AgentPermissionStore.setPermissionPolicy writes directly to UserDefaults.standard via `defaults.set(policy.persistentKey, forKey:)`. This is synchronous. permissionPolicy reads from UserDefaults on each access. SettingsView reads `currentPolicy` in the Menu label which is computed at render time. SwiftUI @Observable pattern on AppState and the view re-rendering on Menu selection ensures the UI updates immediately.

steps.expected: Tests confirm immediate reads after writes
steps.observed: SessionFilterAndSettingsTests: testPermissionPolicyChangesAreImmediate — set autoApproveAll, read autoApproveAll; set autoRejectHighRisk, read autoRejectHighRisk; set promptAlways, read promptAlways. All sequential writes confirmed immediately readable.

steps.action: Check InlineModelSelectorView for conditional Pi/Droid controls
steps.expected: PiThinkingLevelPicker appears only for Pi, DroidAutonomyControl only for Droid
steps.observed: HeaderView.InlineModelSelectorView body includes: `if activeAgentType.isPi { PiThinkingLevelPicker(...) } else if activeAgentType.isDroid { DroidAutonomyControl(...) }`. The activeAgentType is passed from ConversationModelPickerPanel, which uses the selected agent type from AgentSelectionStore. Pi shows thinking level picker, Droid shows autonomy control, Codex/ACP show neither.

steps.action: Verify test coverage for isPi/isDroid properties
steps.expected: Tests confirm isPi/isDroid partition correctly
steps.observed: SessionFilterAndSettingsTests: testPiThinkingLevelOnlyForPiAgents — piNative.isPi=true, piAcp.isPi=true, all others false. testDroidAutonomyOnlyForDroidAgents — droidNative.isDroid=true, droidAcp.isDroid=true, all others false. testAgentSpecificControlsNotShownForCodexOrAcp — confirms neither isPi nor isDroid for Codex and GenericAcp.

codeReferences: apps/ios/Sources/Litter/Views/HeaderView.swift: InlineModelSelectorView — `if activeAgentType.isPi { PiThinkingLevelPicker } else if activeAgentType.isDroid { DroidAutonomyControl }`
apps/ios/Sources/Litter/Views/AgentBadgeView.swift: AgentType extension — isPi, isDroid computed properties

summary: Tested 14 assertions via code review (rg + Read): all 14 pass. All assertions have corresponding production code implementing the required behavior and XCTest unit tests verifying correctness.
